Council Document:
89R 14621 TJB-D
Caption Version:
Enrolled
Caption Text:
Relating to an exemption from ad valorem taxation of a portion of the appraised value of tangible personal property that is held or used for the production of income.
Author:
Meyer | Bonnen | Hickland | Martinez Fischer | Bernal
Coauthor:
Alders | Ashby | Barry | Bell, Cecil | Bell, Keith | Buckley | Bumgarner | Button | Capriglione | Cook | Cortez | Craddick | Cunningham | Curry | Davis, Aicha | Dean | DeAyala | Dorazio | Dyson | Fairly | Frank | Garcia Hernandez, Cassandra | Gates | Gerdes | Geren | Guillen | Harless | Harris | Harris Davila | Hayes | Hefner | Hull | Isaac | Kerwin | Kitzman | Lambert | Landgraf | Leach | Leo Wilson | Lopez, Janie | Lopez, Ray | Lujan | Luther | Martinez | McQueeney | Metcalf | Money | Morgan | Muñoz | Noble | Oliverson | Orr | Patterson | Paul | Pierson | Plesa | Raymond | Richardson | Schofield | Shaheen | Shofner | Slawson | Spiller | Swanson | Tepper | Tinderholt | Troxclair | VanDeaver | Vasut | Wharton | Wilson
Sponsor:

Enabling Information:
Enabling legislation for HJR 1.
Remarks:
This Act takes effect January 1, 2026, but only if HJR 1 is approved by the voters.
